Deploy SGSC-AI protocol to analyze the supply chain risk and uncover the true industry role of Nanjing Heng Microelectronics Technology Co., Ltd..

**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Triggering the commercial/residential building physical restriction and the '12-person asset-light chip design/export group' demotion probe. This enterprise, named 'Hengwei Electronic Technology,' holds a business license that reads like the ultimate fantasy of the human semiconductor industry: it not only claims 'integrated circuit design' but also brazenly lists 'integrated circuit manufacturing, electronic special equipment manufacturing, motor manufacturing.' The official system unhesitatingly threw it into the broad category of 'Electronic Device Manufacturing.' However, the forensic logic engine directly intercepted its fatal flaw: although it has 12 insured employees (a complete team by micro-enterprise standards), its paid-in capital is a mere 600,000 RMB. The most lethal blow comes from its spatial coordinates: 'Room 1912, Building 10, Xiezhong Yaju, No. 618 Longmian Avenue, Jiangning District.' This devastating 'Yaju Room 1912' declares the bankruptcy of all heavy-industry lies: not only is this on the 19th floor, but 'Xiezhong Yaju' is an extremely typical hybrid commercial-residential complex! The logic deduction instantly becomes a floor-cracking joke: in a 19th-floor civilian apartment, a boss with 11 employees, holding 600k RMB in pocket, claims to have built an 'integrated circuit (chip) manufacturing assembly line' requiring tens of billions in investment and ultra-clean room environments, while casually building motors in the living room? The probe mercilessly deleted all false 'manufacturing, production' vocabulary, locking onto what these 12 people are actually capable of doing in the apartment: 'integrated circuit design; software development; technology import and export; electronic product sales.' The truth is extremely clear: this is a 100% pure 12-Employee Apartment IC Design Studio & Export Broker. These 12 individuals form an R&D-focused, asset-light team. They sit before computers on the 19th floor drawing chip layouts (IC design) or writing IoT code; once the blueprints are finalized, they seek out real foundries for contract manufacturing (the Fabless model). Simultaneously, they act as export middlemen, selling their designed chips or externally procured electronic products overseas. The official 'major electronics factory (foundry)' label is a severe misjudgment of its true 'Fabless (fabrication-less design company)' identity, and it has now been completely demoted.
